entry.title: Changing the credit horizon from months to days in P23 system
entry.content:
Hello Team,

In our system we got a request to change the credit horizon from months to days. We would like to know if it is possible to change the credit horizon and what are the implications by changing this horizon and what would action items to be taken if we change credit horizon from months to days.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Changing the credit horizon from months to days in P2...
entry.content:
Hello,
 
The horizon is defined in Customizing (transaction OVA8). The time units correspond to the settings for statistics structure S066 in transaction OMO1.
So to change the time units for credit horizon to days, you need to change the time period split for credit value updates in OMO1 transaction for infostructure S066.
See SAP Note 379007 - Dynamic credit limit check

The setting for infostructure update affects the update for all credit accounts. Also, in order for it to be correctly used and effective, the credit values should be recalculated with report RVKRED77 first.
See SAP Note: 400311 - RVKRED77: Reorganization credit data, new documentation
 
Best regards,
Edina

entry.title: Re: how can I change my Change Dimension Type of my own d...
entry.content:
@tkj 

Not sure of what you are trying to do but the format you show from file is timestamp. You would need this column to be mapped to a timestamp type dimension of your model. Unless you have a timestamp type dimension in your model you cannot load the timestamp information column. Check your model if you have a timestamp type dimension and if not add to it. Date dimension is not same as timestamp as it does not contain the time part in it. You cannot change a date dimension to timestamp.

 

Hope this helps !!

Nikhil

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA会計文脈での拡張ユースケースのご紹介 - 固定資産管理業務におけるSAP Business Technology Platformの活用アイデア -
entry.content:
@2024_Hanami 
Hanami様、返信が遅くなりまして大変失礼いたしました。

前提として、当ブログでご紹介している「固定資産の現物管理機能の拡張開発」のユースケースは、あくまで開発例です。顧客要件によって、自由に開発いただけます。

ご質問いただいた点につきましては、S/4HANAとBTPとの連携部分が実現できればS/4HANA public cloudでも対応可能です。

このシナリオでS/4HANAとBTPが連携しているのは下記の2か所です。その他はBTP上で開発した機能なので、特にS/4HANAによる影響は受けません。

①冒頭、S/4HANAの資産マスタのデータをBTP上に連携するところ
⑤最後、BTP上で回収した棚卸結果をもとに、BTP画面からS/4HANAのトランザクションを呼び出すところ（例：除却のAPI実行、固定資産マスタ画面への遷移など）

以上、ご参考になれば幸いです。

entry.title: Understanding Facade Microservices: Simplifying Complexity in Microservices Architecture
entry.content:
In the ever-evolving landscape of software development, microservice architecture has emerged as a powerful paradigm for building scalable and maintainable applications. Within this framework, the concept of a facade microservice plays a crucial role, acting as a gateway or intermediary that simplifies interactions with a complex system of underlying microservices. This blog will delve into what facade microservices are, how they differ from API gateways, and the benefits they bring, especially in the context of Field Service Management (FSM).

What is a Facade Microservice?

A facade microservice provides a unified interface that conceals the intricate business logic within various subsystems, serving as a singular point of entry or exit. It masks the complexities of internal services, making it easier for clients to interact with the system. This is particularly valuable in microservice architectures, where interactions can become convoluted due to the number of services involved.

Key Functions of Facade Microservices:

  1. Unified Interface: Facade services expose a single API to clients, aggregating multiple service calls into one request. This reduces the complexity of client-side code.
  2. Abstraction: They abstract the implementations of the underlying microservices, allowing clients to interact with a simpler interface without needing to know the details of each service.
  3. Aggregation: Facades can gather responses from multiple backend services, providing a cohesive response to clients—ideal for scenarios requiring information from different sources.
  4. Decoupling: Clients are decoupled from backend changes. If backend services are modified, the facade can adapt without necessitating changes to the client.
  5. Security and Access Control: Facades enforce security policies and manage access control, adding an extra layer of security.
  6. Performance Optimization: They can enhance performance by implementing caching strategies and reducing the number of network calls.

How Do Facade Microservices Differ from API Gateways?

While both facade microservices and API gateways serve as intermediaries within a microservices architecture, they fulfill distinct roles:

Facade Microservices:

  • Purpose: Simplifies interactions with multiple underlying services by providing a unified interface, often incorporating business logic and orchestration of service calls.
  • Functionality: Can aggregate responses from various services, handling complex workflows that require coordination.
  • Client Interaction: Clients interact directly with the facade for specific operations, such as placing an order or retrieving user profiles.

API Gateway:

  • Purpose: Acts as a single entry point for all client requests to backend services, primarily focusing on routing, load balancing, and security.
  • Functionality: Handles cross-cutting concerns like authentication, rate limiting, and logging, routing requests to the appropriate microservices without aggregating responses.
  • Client Interaction: Clients send requests to the API gateway, which then forwards them to the necessary services.

In essence, a facade microservice may utilize an API gateway to route its requests to the underlying services. They complement each other within a microservices architecture.

Example: Facade Microservice in Field Service Management (FSM)

Consider an FSM application that consists of various microservices, each handling specific aspects of field operations, such as:

  • Service Calls Service: Manages service call creation, assignment, and tracking.
  • Booking Service: Optimizes technician schedules and appointments.
  • Skills Service: Tracks technician skills and their validity.

A Field Service Facade Microservice can be created to provide a unified interface for clients (like mobile apps or web applications) to manage these field operations.

How It Works:

  1. Client Request: A dispatcher sends a request to create or update a service call to the Field Service Facade.
  2. Internal Coordination:
    • The facade validates the request and checks technician availability through the booking service.
    • It verifies the technician's skills with the Skills Service, notifying the dispatcher if the technician possesses the required skills.
    • It schedules service calls using the Scheduling Service.
  3. Response: The facade returns a single response to the client, indicating the success of the operation or any issues encountered.

Benefits of Using a Facade Microservice:

  • Simplified client interaction: clients interact with a single service instead of multiple services, streamlining the process.
  • Reduced Complexity: The facade abstracts the complexities of coordinating multiple services, simplifying management.
  • Flexibility: If an underlying service changes, only the facade requires updating, enhancing maintainability and scalability.

When to Use Facade Microservices

Facade microservices are particularly useful in scenarios such as:

  • When dealing with complex systems containing many interdependent microservices.
  • When a simplified API for external clients is necessary.
  • Enforcing security policies and authorization rules is critical.
  • When optimizing performance and resilience is a priority.

Conclusion

Facade microservices are instrumental in simplifying and orchestrating complex interactions between services, often incorporating additional business logic. In contrast, API gateways focus on managing and routing requests, providing a centralized access point for clients while addressing security and other cross-cutting concerns. By understanding the distinct roles of these components, organizations can better design their microservices architectures to enhance maintainability, scalability, and overall efficiency.

entry.title: Re: Convert multiple Rows in a Single row with different columns in ALV Report
entry.content:
I don't know what your exact question is, but maybe you are looking for ASSIGN COMPONENT (classic question in the forum). Even ASSIGN without COMPONENT could be used.

DATA(field_name) = 'PERIOD' && POPER.

ASSIGN COMPONENT field_name OF STRUCTURE alv_line TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<period_quantity>).

<period_quantity> = <period_quantity> + HSL_CRDT.

entry.title: Re: SAP GUI ALV width on 2K, 4K monitors
entry.content:
2541592 - Display issues when using SAP GUI for Windows with high DPI settings has the following information on Hi DPI monitors, 

 

General recommendations / information

  • Use a modern theme starting with Belize theme. These themes have much better high DPI capabilities built-in compared to older themes.
  • If you do not want to use one of the themes as of Belize, use at least Blue Crystal theme, because this theme comes with an icon font that displays all kinds of icons and symbols in a much better way with high DPI. 
  • If you are facing issues apply the latest patch for your SAP GUI for Windows release. We keep fixing display issues in our patches. Therefore the latest patch will potentially solve issues you are facing.
  • With high DPI settings the default monospace font used by SAP GUI for Windows in older themes (Courier New) may look "thin". The same issue also happens in other applications with this font. If this confuses users you can change the SAP GUI font settings so that Courier New is used with style "bold" instead of "regular".

Multi monitor DPI awareness

SAP GUI for Windows 7.60 and older releases do not support multi monitor DPI awareness.
This can lead to the following effects:

  • SAP GUI may look blurry if multiple monitors with different DPI settings are used. This happens, because Windows applies scaling to SAP GUI in these cases.
  • If SAP GUI for Windows is embedded ("inplace") in a program that supports multi monitor DPI awareness (like SAP Business Client or Internet Explorer) the size of the UI elements in SAP GUI for Windows will be wrong (too small or too large). In case of the integration into Internet Explorer or Edge (in Internet Explorer mode) this also affects windows of the same SAP GUI process (saplogon.exe / saplgpad.exe) which are not embedded ("outplace"), such as the SAP Logon window or SAP GUI windows. Once SAP GUI has been started "inplace" these windows will stop reacting to dpi changes at all, which may also result in wrong window and UI element sizes. The only solution to these issues is using Multi-Monitor-Scaling in SAP GUI for Windows (supported as of SAP GUI for Windows 7.70 and requiring at least the Belize theme).”

More information regarding multi monitor DPI awareness in general can be found on the Microsoft page: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-US/windows/desktop/api/shellscalingapi/ne-shellscalingapi-process_dpi_awareness

As of SAP GUI for Windows 7.70, SAP GUI supports multi monitor DPI awareness as an optional feature in modern SAP GUI themes. This solves the issues mentioned above.
See SAP Note 2985351 for more information.

entry.title: RAP Unit Testing: How to Create a Draft Entity Test Double (Managed Scenario)
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a managed RAP entity with a behavior implementation method that tries to read active and draft entities. I already know it works in practice through testing it in my Fiori app.

In my unit test for the class, I mock the entities using the test double framework and call my method, which should be able to read entities inserted into the cds or sql test double frameworks. For active entities, my method works with no problem and is able to get a READ result.

However, for draft entities, I could not find a way to create them using the test double framework and our READ gets no result. I tried to insert the persistent draft table as a dependency in the CDS test environment and to insert it through the sql test environment instead, as well, but this did not result in a READ result in the method.

The only thing that did work was a workaround/hack to insert the data I wanted into the CDS test environment as an active entity and then execute the EDIT method on it.

I also could not find any tutorial or documentation about this. The only thing I found was a blog post where someone used the same workaround as me:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/abap-units-in-abap-restful-programming-model/ba-p/13500251

But I wanted to know if there is a "best practice" for doing this or if it is actually intended to use the EDIT workaround.

Thank you,
Zena

entry.title: SAP Fiori Elements - Issue with EditFlow (onBeforeCreate)
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have a List Report & Object Page application and on click of Create, I want to display a Confirmation popup and then proceed to Object Page for creation. For this purpose, I am using onBeforeCreate method of editFlow.

Whenever there are no entries in the List Report page (meaning no table entries), this method is working as expected.

On click of Proceed, it will open the Object Page for Creation. This is as expected.

But, whenever there are entries in List Report page (meaning - I hit the Go button to fetch the entries from Backend), this flow is not working as expected and it behaves weird.

I have entries in the Table. Now I hit the Create button

It opens the confirmation popup. Before clicking on Proceed, It also opens the Object Page.

The pop-up is auto-closed after few seconds and then Object page keeps buffering forever.

The documentation says the method should return a Promise. I have written logic to resolve and reject promise based on the buttons in the confirmation dialog. As said above, it works as expected when there are no entries in the table.

I am not sure what I am missing here. Can someone please help on this?

@SAPSupport

entry.title: HANA on-premise - Memory error with SELECT COUNT(*) on Calculation View
entry.content:
On Hana2 sp7 we have some large calculation wiews on which it is impossible to execute the count of record like: select count(*) from ‘view name’ because the select ends in memory error with the message (over 300gb of memory consumed which is the current limit of available memory in hana):

Cannot allocate enough memory: search table error: cannot allocate enough memory: [9] Memory allocation failed;exception 1000002: Allocation failed ; $failure_type$=STATEMENT_MEMORY_LIMIT_FROM_GLOBAL_CONFIG

The same error obviously also occurs with the data preview.

The number of records in one of these views is 270 million so not excessive and the model is built on a base table (not partitioned) with these characteristics:

The strange think is that if the select is more complex, for example inserting a group by year like: select count(*),year from ‘view name’ group by year, it works correctly and consumes few memory (30 gb). The same if we add a where to the select(*) on the id field (which is the 'key' field of the view and is never 'null') like: select count(*) from ‘view_name’ where id is not null.

The view's data model has already been optimized and there is nothing more that can be done about it, we also tried to apply different HINTs to the calc. view but without success.

Do you have any ideas on possible causes and any suggestions?

Thanks

Regards

Luca

entry.title: OUT NOW: SAP Signavio November 2024 release
entry.content:
We are excited to announce the general availability of our SAP Signavio November 2024 release. The highlights of this release include new process AI capabilities and Joule integration, improved integrations between SAP Signavio and SAP, and quick performance and progress review with trendlines and new simplified dashboards to boost insights.

The main benefits of this release include the following:

  • Faster insights, enhanced productivity, and seamless interaction with SAP Signavio solutions thanks to new AI and copilot capabilities
  • Improved integrations between SAP Signavio and SAP and harmonized user experience across SAP Signavio suite
  • Align journey revisions with best practices and easily submit for approvals
  • Enhance supply chain process design, transportation logistics and financial closing processes
  • Quick and easy performance and progress review with trendlines and new dashboards
  • Enhanced data extraction and connectivity to Salesforce, Coupa, and Microsoft Dynamics

In this release, we are launching new process AI capabilities including AI-assisted process analyzer, text to insights​ (available via a beta program) as well as the integration of Joule into SAP Signavio. SAP’s AI copilot Joule is now available to SAP Signavio customers in Germany and the US joining a dedicated Early Adopter Care program.

Additionally, we are delivering improved integrations between SAP Signavio and SAP solutions including SAP Cloud ALM, SAP Risk and Assurance Management (RAM) and SAP Transportation Management. The integration of SAP Signavio and SAP Cloud ALM accelerates business process transformations by facilitating the transfer of to-be process models for implementation. The new integration of SAP Signavio with SAP Risk and Assurance Management (RAM) allows for the monitoring of business-critical processes and the execution of compliance checks to ensure their effectiveness while managing associated risks. The SAP Transportation Management (SAP TM) application integrates fleet and logistics management throughout your network, helping to reduce complexity, increase efficiency, and improve agility for a more sustainable, risk-resilient supply chain.

Starting in 2025, we are excited to share a new product release approach that will bring benefits to customers and the product development process. The introduction of continuous delivery will allow for faster delivery of new features, providing customers with quicker access to enhancements and an improved user experience. Importantly, customers will have the option to decide whether to activate each update, providing flexibility to tailor the experience to their needs. These regular updates will facilitate faster bug fixes, prompt issue resolutions, and essential security patches, ensuring a reliable, secure, and user-friendly product experience. Furthermore, the new approach will make the development process more responsive to customer feedback, allowing for quick implementation of changes based on user input. This constant adaptation will help meet customer expectations and enhance overall satisfaction. In conclusion, the ability to deliver new features between major releases signifies a significant improvement in product development, resulting in a better customer experience and a more robust and secure product.

Highlights from the November 2024 release are summarized below:

 

New process AI capabilities

After the successful release of the AI-assisted process recommender and performance indicators recommender in August, new process AI capabilities are now coming your way that will help further accelerate and simplify how you analyze your business processes and interact with SAP Signavio solutions. 

AI-assisted process analyzer, text to insights (beta program)

With the new AI-assisted process analyzer, text to insights capability, users can ask questions in natural language and receive immediate insights that can accelerate business decisions. This capability is designed to democratize process mining as it empowers users with any skillset, regardless of their technical knowledge, to conduct deep analysis by adopting a conversational approach.

The process analyzer, text to insights capability is available via a beta program offered to SAP Signavio Process Intelligence customers. If you are interested in joining the beta program, please contact your SAP Signavio account executive or customer success partner.

Process AI capabilities – AI-assisted process analyzer, text to insightsClick here to start the step-by-step tutorial for this feature

 

Joule integrated into SAP Signavio (Early Adopter Care program)

Joule, the AI copilot for the SAP ecosystem helps users be more efficient and productive through simply describing their ideas, asking questions, or telling the system what to do. As Joule’s integration across SAP’s portfolio continues to grow, so does its unique ability to correlate data spread across your business. 

With the start of the EAC, Joule in SAP Signavio supports early informational, navigational and transactional use cases. Users can now ask Joule how to perform a task and be guided through help documentation (e.g. “tell me how to set up a dashboard”). They can ask Joule to locate specific assets or information within the SAP Signavio Collaboration Hub or SAP Signavio Process Intelligence (“tell me where I can find this diagram”), or request the co-pilot to execute tasks such as creating or deleting a journey model (“do this…”).

Don’t wait  to register, as seats are limited and specific criteria apply. The EAC offers a unique opportunity to adopt new innovations early on, engage in active discussions with SAP experts, and influence the future development of process AI capabilities for SAP Signavio solutions. During this EAC program, Joule in SAP Signavio is available free of charge.

Registration for the EAC program for Joule in SAP Signavio starts today and will remain open until February 28, 2025. Apply here now!

Process AI capabilities – Joule integrated into SAP Signavio (Early Adopter Care program)

 

Process analysis and mining

SAP Signavio Process Insights

With the November 2024 release, SAP Signavio Process Insights is releasing the following highlights:

  • Trend analysis: Trendlines to analyze process flow performance and ability
  • Ability to analyze monetary value over time
  • Personal analysis dashboards
  • External benchmarks for process flows and additional process performance indicators
  • Integration with SAP Transportation Management as a source system
  • Data collection: ability to change default behavior of new content

Trend analysis: Trendlines to analyze process flow performance and ability to analyze monetary value over time

To fully understand your company's process performance, you must be able to review and monitor changes over time. With the introduction of trendlines for process flows, process owners and transformation drivers can identify real progress or shortcomings. With the ability to filter trendlines, users will be able to select specific data sets to see determined patterns and deviations, allowing for deeper insights and more-precise decision-making. Another addition to this area is the ability to analyze monetary value over time. This update empowers process owners to analyze monetary values over time and track back their financial performance, discover trends, and make data-driven decisions. The growing preconfigured business content then offers even more value, allowing multiple stakeholders to collaborate on process performance and accelerate time to analysis.

SAP Signavio Process Insights – Trendlines to analyze process flow performanceClick here to start the step-by-step tutorial for this feature

SAP Signavio Process Insights – Ability to analyze monetary value over timeClick here to start the step-by-step tutorial for this feature

 

Personal analysis dashboards

As part of this release, users will now be able to create personalized dashboards with their most relevant metrics and define parameters of the metrics displayed. SAP Signavio Process Insights includes more than 3,000 metrics, providing a powerful toolkit to analyze your processes and identify the root causes of your business issues. This latest update provides a quick and intuitive way to collect your relevant metrics in one place, where they can be readily accessed again for further analysis.

SAP Signavio Process Insights – Personal analysis dashboards Click here to start the step-by-step tutorial for this feature

 

External benchmarks for process flows and additional process performance indicators

In order to support you in further understanding your own benchmarks for process flows firstly, and we have increased the number of process performance indicators covered as well.

SAP Signavio Process Insights – External benchmarks for process flows and additional process performance indicators

 

Integration with SAP Transportation Management as a source system

The SAP Transportation Management (SAP TM) application integrates fleet and logistics management throughout your network, helping to reduce complexity, increase efficiency, and improve agility for a more sustainable, risk-resilient supply chain. New process performance indicators and process flows are available for SAP Transport Management source systems for planning and fulfillment.

SAP Signavio Process Insights – Integration with SAP Transportation Management as a source systemClick here to start the step-by-step tutorial for this feature

 

Data collection: ability to change default behavior of new content

SAP Signavio Process Insights is constantly releasing new business content. In certain situations, administrators may not want all this content to be immediately available to users. This feature allows you to determine when business content is to be made available to users, either automatically or only when activated manually.

SAP Signavio Process Insights – Data collection: ability to change default behavior of new contentClick here to start the step-by-step tutorial for this feature

 

SAP Signavio Process Intelligence

SAP Signavio Process Intelligence releases the following highlights:

  • Coupa, Microsoft Dynamics CRM, and Salesforce connectivity using SAP Cloud Integration
  • Automated Pagination and Partitioning Strategy ​for SAP ERP Data Extraction
  • Support for SQL-Subqueries and Variables in Delta Filter for Extracting Data from SAP ERP Systems
  • Improved filtering: new Filter UI with drag-and-drop functionality

Coupa, Microsoft Dynamics CRM, and Salesforce connectivity using SAP Cloud Integration

Within this November release, you can now leverage an integration package on SAP Cloud Integration, enabling you to automatically integrate data from Coupa, Microsoft Dynamics CRM, or Salesforce into SAP Signavio Process Intelligence and mine it for your specific use cases.

SAP Signavio Process Intelligence – Coupa, Microsoft Dynamics CRM, and Salesforce connectivity using SAP Cloud Integration

 

Automated Pagination and Partitioning Strategy for SAP ERP Data Extraction

With this new feature, you will be able to experience the power of streamlined data extraction and an enhanced user experience with our automated pagination and partitioning strategy for SAP ERP Data Extraction. This feature paves the way for a unified user flow across various connection types, effectively streamlining workflow for data engineers.

SAP Signavio Process Intelligence – Automated Pagination and Partitioning Strategy for SAP ERP Data ExtractionClick here to start the step-by-step tutorial for this feature

 

Support for SQL-Subqueries and Variables in Delta Filter for Extracting Data from SAP ERP Systems

This feature allows data engineers to define SQL filters within the standard extraction user flow by linking multiple tables in sub-queries during extraction time. This enhancement enables a more efficient data extraction process, ensuring that the necessary information is obtained with precision and speed.

SAP Signavio Process Intelligence – Support for SQL-Subqueries and Variables in Delta Filter for Extracting Data from SAP ERP SystemsClick here to start the step-by-step tutorial for this feature

 

Improved filtering: new Filter UI with drag-and-drop functionality

Enhance your filtering experience with the new, intuitive drag-and-drop filter UI, designed for faster, more advanced data selection. This upgrade delivers faster time-to-insights and facilitates more effective analysis of processes. The redesigned filter UI streamlines the user workflow through a large and flexible drag-and-drop interface, providing an immediate overview of the applied filters for a seamless user experience.

SAP Signavio Process Intelligence – Improved filtering: new Filter UI with drag-and-drop functionalityClick here to start the step-by-step tutorial for this feature

 

Process and Journey Modeling and Process Governance

SAP Signavio Process Manager

We introduce 7 highlights for SAP Signavio Process Manager in this release:

  • Provide to-be process model from SAP Signavio to SAP Cloud ALM
  • Integration of SAP Signavio with SAP Risk and Assurance Management (RAM)
  • Automatically propagate added or deleted process elements in variant management
  • Display of the technical ID for custom attributes in the UI
  • Support Korean currency in SAP Signavio Process Manager
  • BPMC covering the import of executables and assignments from SAP Solution Manager
  • Ability to connect SAP Signavio systems in the managed private cloud (MPC) environment to the BPMC

Provide to-be process model from SAP Signavio to SAP Cloud ALM

The integration of SAP Signavio and SAP Cloud ALM accelerates your business process transformations by facilitating the transfer of to-be process models for implementation. The integration aligns with SAP’s vision of creating a seamless lifecycle integration that supports customers' clean core ERP strategy. The standard integration, available on November 18, 2024, offers a seamless experience through the SAP Signavio user interface. The provision of the to-be process models from SAP Signavio to SAP Cloud ALM bridges the gap between business and IT to enhance collaboration and helps deliver consistent process structures and information across both platforms through synchronized data.

SAP Signavio Process Manager – Provide to-be process model from SAP Signavio to SAP Cloud ALM

 

Integration of SAP Signavio with SAP Risk and Assurance Management (RAM)

The integration of SAP Signavio with SAP Risk and Assurance Management (RAM) allows you to monitor business-critical processes and the execution of compliance checks to ensure their effectiveness while managing associated risks. This comprehensive compliance and risk management capability enhances SAP Signavio by ensuring consistency between process, control, and risk repositories in both SAP Signavio and SAP RAM. Additionally, it reduces the need for manual maintenance, saving both time and effort.

SAP Signavio Process Manager – Integration of SAP Signavio with SAP Risk and Assurance Management (RAM)

 

Automatically propagate added or deleted process elements in variant management

Automate the propagation of added or deleted process elements in variant management to ensure streamlined updates and consistent variants. This approach simplifies variant management by reducing manual effort and maintaining uniformity across processes. With automatic propagation of elements like tasks and systems, synchronization across variants is achieved effortlessly, eliminating the need for manual intervention. This not only saves time but also reduces complexity, leading to more efficient process management. Additionally, a transparent overview of changes allows for easy identification of modifications, facilitating quicker decision-making and enhanced collaboration.

SAP Signavio Process Manager – Automatically propagate added or deleted process elements in variant management

Display of the technical ID for custom attributes in the UI

Take advantage of an enhanced UI in SAP Signavio Process Manager, now displaying technical IDs for custom attributes of processes, tasks, and dictionary items – effectively addressing the issue of inaccessible IDs. This improvement reduces reliance on consulting services, encourages user adoption, and minimizes unauthorized workarounds. By making technical IDs readily visible, users can easily retrieve and configure custom field names, empowering them to manage custom attribute IDs independently. This independence not only decreases the need for external support and promotes scalable adoption, but also enhances compliance with governance with governance policies by providing an authorized method to access these IDs. Overall, this enhancement streamlines the configuration of custom fields, saving time and effort in integration and configuration tasks.

SAP Signavio Process Manager – Display of the technical ID for custom attributes in the UI

Support Korean currency in SAP Signavio Process Manager

SAP Signavio Process Manager now supports the Korean Won (KRW) in simulations and reporting, significantly enhancing its global reach and enabling users in Korea to conduct precise simulations and generate accurate reports in their local currency. This feature addresses the specific needs of the Korean market, fostering better integration, informed decision-making, and operational efficiency for businesses in the region. By enabling localized financial simulations, organizations can improve planning accuracy and make decisions based on reliable local currency data. Additionally, this enhancement streamlines financial processes, saving time and reducing errors, while the tailored functionalities for the Korean market elevate user satisfaction and drive increased adoption among Korean businesses.

SAP Signavio Process Manager – Support Korean currency in SAP Signavio Process Manager

 

BPMC covering the import of executables and assignments from SAP Solution Manager

The enhanced business process model connector for SAP Signavio Process Manager enables seamless synchronization of your executables and task assignments from SAP Solution Manager to SAP Signavio Process Manager. This allows SAP Signavio Process Manager to accurately represent the use of system transactions in the process, reducing manual effort and improving tracking and compliance.

SAP Signavio Process Manager – BPMC covering the import of executables and assignments from SAP Solution Manager

Ability to connect SAP Signavio systems in the managed private cloud (MPC) environment to the BPMC

The business process model connector now enables seamless integration with managed private cloud (MPC) environments, allowing customers of SAP Signavio Process Manager to fully leverage its capabilities to meet advanced security requirements. By selecting MPC as the designated data center when adding a system to the business process model connector, you can enhance your operational efficiency while ensuring robust data security.

SAP Signavio Process Manager – Ability to connect SAP Signavio systems in the managed private cloud (MPC) environment to the BPMC

 

SAP Signavio Journey Modeler and SAP Signavio Process Governance

As part of this November release for SAP Signavio Journey Modeler and SAP Signavio Process Governance, we are releasing the following highlight:

Journey governance: automated approvals

Journey modelers are now empowered with the robust automated approvals feature in SAP Signavio Journey Modeler and SAP Signavio Process Governance. This structured approval mechanism ensures that journey revisions align with organizational best practices and comply with relevant regulations. Modelers can effortlessly submit their revisions for approval to designated journey owners and key stakeholders, streamlining both the revision and compliance processes. In addition, modelers can also request approvals for new journey models or changes, ensuring a robust audit trail and streamlined tasks. This allows for real-time updates on model approval status changes, streamlines collaboration and increases accountability while saving valuable time.

SAP Signavio Journey Modeler – Journey governance: automated approvals

 

Process transformation management and collaboration

SAP Signavio Collaboration Hub

With the November 2024 release for SAP Signavio Collaboration Hub, we are releasing the following highlight:

Fact Sheet for process model diagrams

Enhance process documentation and analysis in your organization with the new, predefined fact sheet for process models in SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub. This feature provides a centralized, detailed, intuitively accessed overview of process diagrams, consolidating key information and insights in one place. With the fact sheet, users can quickly understand and analyze process diagrams and better understand processes. Detailed overview of each process diagram, including key metrics, descriptions, and related documentation for a process model, with the following predefined and default detail cards:

  • Process description and objective
  • Process input and process output
  • Suppliers and Customers
  • Activities
  • IT systems
  • Documents
  • Roles
  • Risks

Administrator ability to configure the following in the settings of SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub:

  • Enabling and disabling Fact Sheet for process models
  • Making Fact Sheet as default view for the process model diagram
  • Setting the visibility and order of the cards to be consumed in Fact Sheet

SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub– Fact Sheet for process model diagramsClick here to start the step-by-step tutorial for this feature

 

SAP Signavio Process Transformation Manager

Within SAP Signavio Process Transformation Manager, we are releasing the following highlights:

Task notifications

With the real-time in-app and e-mail task notifications, you never miss an update and can take timely action. Be it within the app or in your inbox, task notifications will keep you informed, engaged, and productive. Stay in the loop and accelerate your business growth with SAP Signavio Process Transformation Manager.

SAP Signavio Process Transformation Manager – Task notifications

 

Extended access management for insights

Enhance the sharing and collaboration of Insights and Initiatives through targeted and transparent user access management. The improved visibility into who has access allows you to easily share insights with individuals, user groups or based on existing data permissions. It enables seamless collaboration on insights captured within SAP Signavio Process Transformation Manager, ensuring the right users have the right access to drive successful transformations.

SAP Signavio Process Transformation Manager – Extended access management for insightsClick here to start the step-by-step tutorial for this feature

 

SAP Signavio Value Accelerators

We are excited to announce that the following new value accelerators are now available for you to speed up your business process modeling and transformation initiatives as part of this November 2024 release:

Value accelerator library for SAP Signavio solutions:

System Filter

Many use cases for value accelerators are determined by the systems they support. You can now streamline your search by using the system filter, which directly incorporates your selected systems of interest. This feature eliminates the need to sift through packages to determine compatibility, significantly reducing the time to value by quickly identifying the appropriate value accelerator.

Value accelerator library for SAP Signavio solutions – System FilterClick here to start the step-by-step tutorial for this feature

 

SAP Signavio Process Intelligence value accelerator:

Value Accelerator for Aligning Demand, Supply, and Financial Plans in SAP Integrated Business Planning for Supply Chain

The new value accelerator for aligning demand, supply, and financial plans in SAP Integrated Business Planning for Supply Chain enables consultants and data engineers to achieve faster results with less effort and clear guidance for analysis. It facilitates process analysis in SAP Integrated Business Planning and provides a templated approach for quick implementation, allowing for customizations based on individual needs. Key features include prebuilt data transformation templates and a metrics template, empowering users to enhance processes supported by SAP Integrated Business Planning for Supply Chain.

SAP Signavio Process Intelligence – Value Accelerator for Aligning Demand, Supply, and Financial Plans in SAP Integrated Business Planning for Supply Chain

 

SAP Signavio Process Insights value accelerators:

Value Accelerator for SAP Transportation Management

The SAP Transportation Management (SAP TM) application integrates fleet and logistics management across your network, reducing complexity, increasing efficiency, and enhancing agility for a more sustainable and resilient supply chain. New usage process performance indicators and process flows will be introduced for SAP TM source systems to support plan fulfillment. This will provide fact-based insights into how SAP TM is utilized, enabling quicker access to insights regarding your process performance.

SAP Signavio Process Insights – Value Accelerator for SAP Transportation Management

 

Value Accelerator for Logistics Execution Transportation (LE-TRA)

Logistics Execution Transportation (LE-TRA) enables efficient planning and execution of shipments, as well as the settlement of associated costs. This feature helps reduce transportation-related expenses and enhances on-time delivery. Gain fact-based insights into your shipments, allowing you to understand which shipments and cost items are processed accurately within the SAP ERP system.

SAP Signavio Process Insights – Value Accelerator for Logistics Execution Transportation (LE-TRA)

Value Accelerator for Logistics Execution Warehouse Management (LE-WM)

Logistics Execution Warehouse Management (LE-WM) enables the planning and execution of transfer requirements and orders within a straightforward warehouse environment. This feature helps reduce warehouse-related costs and enhance on-time delivery. It provides fact-based insights into transfer requirements and orders, allowing you to assess which ones are accurately processed in warehouse management and identify the locations of slow-moving stock.

SAP Signavio Process Insights – Value accelerator for purchasing with service entry sheets

 

Partnerships and Integrations

KYP.ai now available on the SAP Store as Validated Partner

KYP.ai is a productivity intelligence solution that continuously captures operational data and identifies how your workforce, processes, and technologies can best work together to perform at peak performance.

Their solution Productivity Intelligence 360 is now available on SAP Store as a validated partner for SAP Signavio. It integrates task-level data and insights on user activity and then, identifies process, task, or workforce improvements through automation, AI, or other optimizations.

Together, KYP.ai and SAP Signavio provide you the visibility necessary to understand and improve processes, technologies, and how people contribute to overall performance. With this partnership, you can access real-time and historical analysis of user productivity. The solution automatically evaluates business cases for automation and ensures comprehensive process compliance across all applications, providing a level of visibility that extends beyond conventional process mining. For more information, please read this blog post on SAP Community.

Partnerships - KYP.ai and SAP Signavio partnership

 

This blog post only provides an overview of the latest features and enhancements of SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite with our August 2024 release. For more details, please read our release notes.  

Please feel free to share your questions around the release or feedback in the comments with us. You can also reach out to us via the SAP Signavio Community.

entry.title: Re: Feature Request: Allow formatting for DURATION functi...
entry.content:
Hello Anna, 

today's release will bring a more fine granular representation of the duration values, even down to the millisecond. 

As suggested by Sam Gosling, the function DURATION_TO_DAYS or DURATION_TO_MILLISECONDS can be used.

 

SELECT

DURATION_TO_DAYS(

AVG (((

SELECT LAST (END_TIME) - FIRST (END_TIME)

)))

)  AS "Cycle time"

FROM THIS_PROCESS

 

This will result in a float number. However, this will basically just aggregate the milliseconds of the duration to days ( 123 Milliseconds / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24)). Therefore, weekends won't be deducted here. If this is the requirement, the calculation would look more complex. 

Best regards

Jessica

entry.title: SAP E-Mobility: Pioneering Solution for Sustainable EV Charging and Charging Network Management
entry.content:
The rise of electric mobility is accelerating across the automotive industry. As the demand for charging stations and efficient management of charging infrastructure grows, intelligent software solutions are becoming increasingly important.

 AI Generated ImageAI Generated Image

SAP E-Mobility offers an integrated platform to help businesses manage and optimize their e-charging infrastructure. A key focus of this solution is the integration with corporate business processes and charging networks like Gireve. 

In the recent IDC MarketScape: Worldwide Electric Vehicle Charging Management Solutions 2024 Vendor Assessment (doc #US49435523, October 2024) report, SAP is recognized as a Major Player in the field of e-mobility solutions.  Ensuring enterprise-grade security level operation, compliance to global regulations, flexible tarif management, managing power grid limitations with smart charging and scaling handling of charging session processing, are differentiating key characteristics of SAP E-Mobility.  

Integration of SAP E-Mobility and Gireve

Gireve is a European roaming network that facilitates access to electric vehicle charging stations. The recent renewal of SAP’s partnership with Gireve allows charging infrastructure operators to market their charging infrastructure services on a broader platform, giving end customers access to a Europe-wide network of charging points. By integrating with Gireve, SAP is expanding its reach, enabling greater coverage of public charging infrastructure for CPOs and eMSPs and increase the utilization of their charging points to driver higher margin.

Flexible Charging Tariffs: A Key to Optimization

Another crucial aspect of SAP E-Mobility is the ability to provide flexible charging session data. This flexibility is particularly relevant for meeting diverse customer needs while ensuring grid stability. E-mobility and charging service companies are enabled to implement dynamic pricing models based on factors like time of day, grid load, or energy tariff. This helps shape charging behavior and incentivizes users to charge their vehicles during off-peak times. These pricing models are not only economically advantageous but also contribute to sustainability by promoting more efficient use of energy resources.

Conclusion

With SAP E-Mobility, SAP provides a future-oriented platform that gives charging infrastructure operators and businesses in the electric mobility sector a competitive edge. Integration with networks like Gireve and the implementation of flexible charging tariffs enhance user experience and grid stability. At the same time, SAP E-Mobility’s positioning as a Major Player in the IDC MarketScape report underscores its innovation and leadership in this field. In a time when sustainability and digitalization go hand in hand, SAP E-Mobility will play a central role in expanding electric mobility.

entry.title: SAP CDP cannot properly ingest CDC custom data array
entry.content:
We have a CDC ingestion connector defined in CDP and we configured the "Get a new or updated account in real time" event which retrieves accounts from CDC.

In CDC we created a custom object in the data schema where we store some data in the following format:

{
   "data":{
      "someArray":[
         {
            "prop1":"3123",
            "prop2":"3ds123"
         },
         {
            "prop1":"6542",
            "prop2":"325ds1"
         }
      ]
   }
}

The problem we have is with the CDC - CDP connector. The event schema, which is automatically generated by CDP and cannot be modified, does not detect that this field should be considered as an array (left side of the screenshot) and no matter what it always imports only the first object from the array in CDC. Even if in CDP customer schema we defined an array of objects (right side of the screenshot).

How can we update the model so it considers "someArray" filed as an array of objects? It automatically works e.g. for phones (screenshot below) or emails default arrays, but it doesn't for custom data fields we create.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: 在 SAP S/4HANA Cloud 中通过自定义逻辑限制修改已被采购订单参考的采购申请
entry.content:
问题背景：

有客户提出需求，在修改采购申请时，如果这个采购申请已经有被采购订单参考，则不允许修改这个采购申请。本文即对该问题展开分析，探索解决方案。

解决方案：

尝试通过自定义逻辑，写一个 BAdI 来满足需求。

分析步骤：

1. 首先，我们需要在 SAP S/4HANA Cloud 系统的应用 – 自定义逻辑里，新增增强实施。根据业务场景，选择下图中的业务上下文以及业务加载项描述，填写好实施描述以及实施标识。

业务上下文：MM_PURREQN_ITEM

业务加载项描述：检查采购申请项目

2. 在自定义逻辑里新增增强实施完成后，我们开始分析问题。根据问题，我们需要限制两个条件。条件一，采购申请处于被编辑而非被创建。条件二，被编辑的采购申请是否有被采购订单参考。我们需要在 CDS Views 里进行查找和检索。对于条件一，我们找到 CDS View I_PurchaseRequisitionItemAPI01。我们根据 BAdI 的 importing parameters 里提供的字段 purchaserequisition 和 purchaserequisitionitem 在该 CDS View 中定位当前采购申请。如果在这个 CDS View 里检索结果不为空，则说明当前采购申请已被创建。对于条件二，我们找到 CDS View I_PurchaseOrderItemAPI01 。同理，使用 BAdI 的 importing parameters 里提供的字段 purchaserequisition 和 purchaserequisitionitem 在 I_PurchaseOrderItemAPI01 中检索。如果在这个 CDS View 里检索结果不为空，则说明存在采购订单参考了当前采购申请，我们需要返回一条 Error Message 来阻止业务流程。

3. 根据以上分析过程，实现 BAdI ，具体代码如下。

 

    DATA:
      lv_po_exists       TYPE abap_bool,
      lo_message         LIKE LINE OF messages,
      ls_item            TYPE mmpur_s_pr_item_import,
      ls_po_item         TYPE I_PurchaseOrderItemAPI01.

    LOOP AT purchaserequisitionitem_table INTO ls_item.

      DATA(purchaserequisition_item_guid) = ls_item-purchaserequisitionitem.

      SELECT SINGLE *
        FROM I_PurchaseRequisitionItemAPI01
        WHERE PurchaseRequisition = @PURCHASEREQUISITION-purchaserequisition
          AND PurchaseRequisitionItem = @LS_item-purchaserequisitionitem
        INTO @DATA(lv_pr_item).

      IF lv_pr_item IS NOT INITIAL.

        SELECT SINGLE *
          FROM I_PurchaseOrderItemAPI01
          WHERE PurchaseRequisition = @PURCHASEREQUISITION-purchaserequisition
            AND PurchaseRequisitionItem = @LV_pr_item-purchaserequisitionitem
          INTO @LS_po_item.

        IF sy-subrc = 0.
          lv_po_exists = abap_true.
        ENDIF.

      ENDIF.

      IF lv_po_exists = abap_true.
        lo_message-messagetype = 'E'.
        lo_message-messagevariable1 = 'This PR cannot be edited.'.
        APPEND lo_message TO messages.
        purchaserequisitionhaserror = abap_true.
        RETURN.
      ENDIF.

    ENDLOOP.

 

4. 我们来校验 BAdI 是否生效。已经在系统中创建了两个采购申请，10001640 和 10001641 ，参考 10001640 创建了采购订单 4500000516 。

尝试对两个采购申请进行修改，结果如图所示。采购申请 10001640 无法被修改，有错误信息产生。采购申请 10001641 可以被修改。说明 BAdI 生效。

 

 

结语：

通过阅读本文，希望您已知晓在为采购订单预制发票时，如何通过自定义逻辑来检查预制发票的付款条件与供应商主数据中的付款条件是否一致。

关注以下链接可以看到更多有关 SAP S/4HANA Cloud 采购相关的问题：All Questions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Sourcing and Procurement | SAP Community

您也可以通过后面的链接阅读更多有关 SAP S/4HANA Cloud 采购的博文：SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Sourcing and Procurement | SAP | SAP Blogs

如果您对本文有任何的建议和想法，欢迎在评论区提出，期待与您一起交流。也欢迎您关注我的个人账号，期待我们在下一篇博文再见！

entry.content:

entry.title: How-To: Use Script Tasks in SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Motivation

SAP Build Process Automation just got even more powerful with its latest feature that allows users to write executable code in JavaScript. This new feature, introduced in October 2024, enables users to perform specific calculations or data manipulations as part of their business process.

Previously, the ability to write executable code was limited to automation tasks, but now, users have access to the entire context of their business process. This means you can manipulate data and perform calculations at various stages of the process, making it more dynamic and responsive to changing needs.

Creating a business project from scratch may seem like a daunting task, but with the right starting point and guidance, it can be a seamless process. In this blog post, I will guide you through the steps to create a business project that includes an action task, a script task, and an approval form to facilitate a simple approval workflow.

Scenario

Imagine a sales team that wants to offer tailored discounts to repeat customers. They need to present these discount requests to the finance department for review and approval, ensuring discounts align with business policy. Here’s how this process typically unfolds:

  • The Sales Team Initiates a Discount Request: They select products and propose discounts based on the customer’s purchase history.
  • Data Fetching: Relevant product information is fetched from the backend.
  • Approval by Finance: The finance team reviews the request and either approves or declines the proposed discounts.

Note: The simplified implementation of this scenario is designed to showcase the usage of a script task within a business process.

Prerequisites

  • Access to Business Technology Platform Admin Console (Trial can be accessed here)
  • Access to SAP Build Process Automation
  • Access to SAP Build Lobby       
  • Access to OData Northwind service 

Getting Started

Let’s break down the scenario into its components and examine how to create the necessary artifacts.

Step 1: Create a SAP BTP Destination

The data required for this process will be retrieved from the OData Northwind service, which offers access to a sample database with product and customer information. We need to set up a SAP BTP Destination that will be utilized by an Action Task in the SBPA project. 

  1. Go to BTP Admin Cockpit and create a new Destination and maintain the properties below in the table.
    PropertyValue
    sap.applicationdevelopment.actions.enabledtrue
    sap.processautomation.enabledtrue
    sap.build.usage

    odata_gen

  2. Name the destination e.g. Northwind
  3. Use the Northwind OData URL: https://services.odata.org/northwind/northwind.svc/
  4. Maintain Authentication as NoAuthentication. After creation the destination looks like below:

Step 2: Create, Test and Publish an Action Project in SBPA

In this step, you will create an Action Project that utilizes the BTP destination. This Action Project will be published to the action library and will be accessible in the SAP Build lobby. Later, this Action Project will be used to retrieve products from the OData Northwind service.

  1. In the SAP Build Lobby, under Connectors node choose Actions and click on Create
  2. Select OData Destinations
  3. Select the BTP Destination (Northwind) that we previously created and choose Products in the available entities and methods of the OData API. Then click Next
  4. Update the Project name and Description or let it as it is. Click Create                                             
  5. The expected output does not match the actual output according to the OpenAPI specifications. We need to change the UnitPrice Type from Number to String
  6. Save your work

 

Step 3: Test, Release and Publish your Action Project

In the Actions Editor you can choose the methods that you would like to publish as Actions. Choose Get entities from Products.

Before releasing, you can test the Action Project. You should receive a list of products from the OData Northwind service.

  • Inputs and Outputs can be customized or simplified as needed, but in this simple example, proceed without making any changes.
  • Switch to the test tab to test the action. Notice that the destination is pre-selected as the action was created based on a destination.
  • Click on test. A successful 200:OK response from the Northwind OData API is now displayed at the bottom of the screen.

 

  1. Click on the Release button at the top right and then click on Publish. “Release” creates a non modifiable version of the Action and “Publish” publishes the Action into SAP Build library from where it can be consumed in different Build Artifacts.
  2. Now, the destination needs to be made accessible in SAP Build Process Automation. Go to Control Tower --> Destinations in the SAP Build Lobby and add the Northwind Destination to your SAP Build Process Automation environments.

 

Step 4: Create a new Business Process Project

  1. SAP Build Lobby -> Create -> Build an Automated Process -> Business Process -> Enter Project name and Description -> Click Create
  2. Click Create Process -> Enter Name -> Click Create
  3. Click on the (+) icon in the middle of the process -> Actions -> Browse all actions
  4. Search for the Action “Get entities from Products” and click on Add

  5. Select the added Action in the Process Canvas and create a destination variable with any name. This variable will be used during deployment of the process to link to an actual destination. A destination variable enables linking different destinations in different landscapes ( Dev, Test, Prod) without changing the Process.

    .

 

Step 5: Create an API Trigger

We are going to use an API Trigger to initiate the workflow. In the Process Editor click on Add a Trigger select API Trigger add a name and click Create. 

  1. Add an API trigger to your process
  2. In the Process Builder click on the blank canvas. Choose the Variables tab and select Configure for the Process Inputs section.
  3. To start the process we need to two variables. Add input variables productitems and discount.
  4. Choose the Variables tab and select Configure for the Custom Variables section. Configure the following variables
    NameType
    itemresultsget_Products_200_output_schema
    totalpriceNumber
    totaldiscountpriceNumber
  5. Select Apply to finish and Save                                                               

 

Step 6: Add and configure the Action Project

  1. To add the Action Project, click on the (+) icon after the Trigger
  2. Select Action
  3. In the Browse library page, find the Action project with name Get entities from Products and click on Add.
  4. Select the Get entities from Products action task. In the General tab select + Create Destination Variable.
  5. A dialog opens up to create a destination variable. Enter the following values and click Create.
    Input FieldInput Value
    IdentifierNorthwind
    TypeSelect Destination

  6. We want to restrict the number of items from the results that come from the OData Northwind service. Therefore we use the productitems variable. Choose the Input tab and add productitems as value for $top:                                                                                                                                                            

  7. Save your work.

 

Step 7: Create a Script Task

We are now incorporating a simple logic into our process. Our goal is to calculate the total price and the total discounted price for products obtained from the OData Service.

  1. In the Process Editor click on (+) icon after the Action Task. Add a Script Task

  2. Double click on the Script Task to open the Editor. Delete the code and add the following code:

 

 

$.context.custom.totalprice = 0;
var result = $.context.action_get_Products_1.result.value;
var discountprice = 0;

result.forEach(function (value) {
    $.context.custom.totalprice += Number(value.UnitPrice);
})

discountprice = ($.context.custom.totalprice * ($.context.startEvent.discount / 100));

$.context.custom.totaldiscountprice =  $.context.custom.totalprice - Number(discountprice);

 

 

You have the option to test your JavaScript code locally by using sample values for the context variables, all without having to deploy the project. To do this, simply switch to the Test Variable tab and input default values for the context variables that are being used. For instance:

Variable NameVariable Value
$.context.action_get_Products_1.result.value[{"CategoryID": 1,"Discontinued": false,"SupplierID": 1,"UnitPrice":"18.0000","ProductName": "Chai","UnitsOnOrder": 0,"QuantityPerUnit": "10 boxes x 20 bags","ProductID": 1,"ReorderLevel": 10,"UnitsInStock": 39},
{"CategoryID": 1,"Discontinued": false,"SupplierID": 1,"UnitPrice":"19.0000","ProductName": "Chang","UnitsOnOrder": 40,"QuantityPerUnit": "24 - 12 oz bottles","ProductID": 2,"ReorderLevel": 25,"UnitsInStock": 17}]
$.context.custom.totalprice0
$.context.startEvent.discount25
$.context.custom.totaldiscountprice0

 

Click Run test

You can find more information about testing: here

Click on Apply and Save

 

Step 8: Create a Approval Form

In the last step, we will design a simple approval form to either approve or reject the product discount.

  1. Navigate back to the Process Builder canvas to create an Approval Form. Click on (+) after the Script Task.
  2. Enter the name as Approval Form and click Create.
  3. Select Approval Form and under General Select Open Editor.
  4. Design the form by dragging and dropping the corresponding form elements as shown below.
    Form Fields ValueField TypeConfiguration (Read Only)
    Approve Products and DiscountHeadline1 x
    A new approval task has been received. Please review and confirm whether the discount can be met or not.Paragraph x
    Product listTable x
    Product NameText x
    Unit PriceText x
    PricesTable x
    Discount %Number x
    Total PriceNumber x
    Total Discount PriceNumber x
    Message to SellerText Area 
  5. The Approval form should be the same as below 
  6. Save the form.
  7. Back in the Process Editor, click on the Approval Form and configure the Subject and Recipients.

    In the Subject section:

    • Enter the subject as: Please review the discounts
    • In the Recipients/Users section, enter "Process Started By".
  8. Configure the inputs of Approval Form. Navigate to Inputs and map the fields accordingly.

  9. Save your work

Congratulations on successfully completing your project! Your process should look like below:                                                                                     

 

Step 9: Release the Business Process Project

In order to start the process, you must first release the business process project and then deploy it. Releasing the project creates a version or snapshot of the changes, while deploying the project makes it available in runtime for use. It is only possible to deploy a released version of the project, and at any given time, there can be multiple deployed versions of the same project.

  1. In the Process Builder, to release a project, click Release button on the top-right corner of the screen and provide a description in the popup dialog. If you are releasing for the first time, then the version will start with 1.0.0. Next time you release, the version numbers will be automatically updated.
  2. Click Release                                                                                  
  3. Once the project is released successfully, click Deploy button on the top-right corner of the screen.
  4. Select the environment and click Deploy.
  5. Select the destination Northwind from the drop down and click Deploy.

 

Step 10: Run the Business Process

  1. Let us test the API Trigger in the Monitoring tab of the SAP Build. Click on the Manage > Process and Workflows tile.
  2. Search for your project.
  3. Click on Start New Instance.
  4. Remove the example payload in the dialog. Use the following JSON in the dialog. 

 

 

{
  "productitems": 6,
  "discount": 35
}​

 

 

  •   Click Start New Instance and Close.

 

Step 11: Accessing the task

Now it's time to monitor the process flow and access the tasks.

  1. Tasks are the request for the users to participate in an approval or review process. These tasks appear in the My Inbox application shipped with SAP Build. Users can claim, approve and reject the task from their inbox. You can add a message to the requester.

  2. Users can approve and reject the task from their inbox and you can add a message to the requester. Additionally, you could consider adding more steps to the process, such as sending a notification email to the requester.                                                                                     

 

Additional Read

To learn more about the topic covered in this blog post, please also refer to the following assets & follow us on SAP community.

  • Related Blog Posts
    • Consume an OData API as Actions using a Destination
    • Transform Data with Javascript in SAP Build Process Automation
  • SAP Help guides for more details
    • https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/create-and-configure-script-task
  • Learning 
    • https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/spa-custom-variables..html

entry.title: replace BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT in called transaction
entry.content:
Hello!

I need to use call a transaction. In that transaction, there are is a BAPI used (BAPI_MATERIAL_SAVEDATA).
Right after that, there is a BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT.

My problem is that after a commit work (what is executed within BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT), a call transaction is terminated and the user will thrown back into the original transaction.

I managed to bypass that by using call function 'DB_COMMIT'.
But now, this doesn't work.

I checked SM12. There are some tables locked.
When I execute the code with the original BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT, I saw that the locks are also resolved.

What I tried then is DEQUEUE_ALL. The locks are gone this way, what now the called BAPI doesn't work. The material is not added like it is, when BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT is used.

I am going crazy.
Any ideas? =/

  call function 'BAPI_MATERIAL_SAVEDATA'
    exporting
      headdata     = lv_mathead
    tables
      extensionin  = lt_extensionin
      extensioninx = lt_extensioninx.
  if sy-subrc ne 0.
    message id sy-msgid type sy-msgty number sy-msgno
          with sy-msgv1 sy-msgv2 sy-msgv3 sy-msgv4.
    call function 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_ROLLBACK'.

  else.
*      call function 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT'.

    call function 'DB_COMMIT'.
    wait up to 3 seconds.

    call function 'DEQUEUE_ALL'.

  endif.

entry.title: Special GL
entry.content:
Hi SAP Guru,

I have a query regarding special GL transactions.

I have created one special Gl for eg. 123 against recon account for eg. 456. So now when i m posting the special GL transaction using tcode F-64 the retention GL is showing both the debit and credit line. However my client wants that the debit line should hit the retention GL as they need to hold the money and credit line should hit the reconciliation account.

As per my understanding it is not possible as when we do the entry the recon account is selected automatically and both debit and credit line uses same GL.

Please advise if this is possible or not.

As tech professionals, we understand the importance of staying ahead in the constantly evolving tech industry. Thanks to the cloud, SAP is innovating every day – and we need (and want to) keep up with the speed. That’s not only true for newcomers in the field, but also for well-versed professionals like SAP Mentors and SAP Champions. Listen to the top influencers, contributors, and advocates within the SAP ecosystem and learn why you should invest in continuous learning, SAP skills, and certification now, with this new video series: 

The first video, "Stay Ahead with Certifications and Continuous Learning," features SAP Mentors emphasizing the importance of continuous learning and certifications in keeping up with the fast-paced tech industry. They share their own experiences and insights on how SAP Certifications have helped them stay ahead in their careers.

"Empowering Your Digital Transformation with SAP" delves into the topic of digital transformation and how SAP is at the forefront of enabling companies to prepare for and embrace this transformation. The SAP Mentors share their insights on the importance of aligning with SAP's digital transformation strategy and how it can drive success for businesses in today's digital age.

In the next video, "Leveraging AI Resources to Drive Innovation and Efficiency," SAP Mentors discuss the role of AI in driving innovation and increasing efficiency within the SAP ecosystem, providing valuable advice on leveraging these resources effectively.  

To wrap up, "Unlocking Potential with SAP Learning Hub" focuses on the power of SAP Learning Hub in providing valuable resources and training for tech professionals. The Mentors share their thoughts on how SAP Learning Hub has played a crucial role in their professional development and how it can benefit others in the SAP community.

entry.title: Re: replace BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT in called transaction
entry.content:
The COMMIT WORK (online help) is required to triger update, release locks, and raise a TRANSACTION_FINISHED event.

You cannot remove it.

To prevent  processing terminaton you could use the statement CALL TRANSACTION USING and set component RACOMMIT of the structure CTU_PARAMS, passed to the statement OPTIONS FROM.

entry.title: Re: Date conversion directly in CDS View entity
entry.content: With date 10/10/2024 (MM/DD/YYYY) EXTRACT_DAY will return 10, the ADD_DAYS with days = minus 10 will return 09/30/2024

entry.title: Re: change the payment conditions
entry.content:
Hi,

Actually base of the payment condition is transaction wise and it is not affected master based.
If you want to changed based on master then all old payment condition also changed and it is missguided to management.
In that case you should check open contracts and applied mass change.

Thanks with regards,
Raju Mane

entry.title: Re: Date conversion directly in CDS View entity
entry.content:
Hi tafkap,

to calculate the last day of the previous month you can try this (aedat is the given date):

 

DATS_ADD_DAYS( cast( concat( concat( SUBSTRING (aedat,1,4), SUBSTRING (aedat,5,2) ), '01' ) as abap.dats ),-1,'NULL') as last_day_prev_month

 

 Full example:

 

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZFI_V_TST'
@AbapCatalog.compiler.compareFilter: true
@AbapCatalog.preserveKey: true
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK
@EndUserText.label: 'Test'
define view ZFI_CV_TST as select from prps {
  aedat,
  DATS_ADD_DAYS( cast( concat( concat( SUBSTRING (aedat,1,4), SUBSTRING (aedat,5,2) ), '01' ) as abap.dats ),-1,'NULL') as last_day_prev_month
}

entry.title: how to pass paremeter in creation mode when navigate from other apps?
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We have a requirement to open (or navigate to) the redeem point app in creation mode from the member app in RAP. The challenge is how to pass the member ID value from the member app to the member ID field in the redeem app in creation mode, so that the end user does not need to re-enter the member ID in the redeem app.

I have tried the navigation functionality, and it works for display/edit mode via key fields or the selection option filter field in the list report page. However, it does not work for creation mode.

How can we pass the value of the member ID field to the redeem point app in creation mode?

Clicking the Redeem Point button in the member app should navigate to the redeem point app in creation mode.

In the the redeem point app, the member id field should be filled with member id from the member apps.

At the Redeem Point screen after navigation, the URL contains the member code value. Is it possible to retrieve the member code value from the URL in the create action of the default function? Alternatively, is there any way to automatically populate the member ID field in the Redeem Point app from the member app?

Many thanks,

Andy

entry.title: Re: Change default delivery address of plant when creatin...
entry.content:
Hi @zoran1512 
We can modify the delivery address of a PO at Item level. PO > Item > 'Delivery Address' tab.
If the address is not editable, we may create a new storage location with required address.
While creating a PO, we need to enter plant along with storage location.
Please let us know if you are facing any Issues on the given suggestions by me and others.
